comments: Missing maybe last 10 mins of show.
Features a BBC Alexis Korner show promo spot.
TV mentions Saxon are on tour, Manchester tomorrow (31st May), and an edited version of the track played will be released as a single.

track_listing.track: '747 (Strangers In The Night)' (LP-Wheels Of Steel) Carrere
'Young Girl Blues' (LP-Nine On A Ten Scale) Capitol

track_listing.details: 12"-The Death & Destiny EP Featuring 'Killer' 12" single re-issue on Street Beat

transcript.text: On 275 and 285. Also in stereo on VHF. This is National Radio 1.
Well, hello there. TV on the radio here. Thomas the Vance. And welcome once again to the Friday Rock Show.
And this evening we repeat 41 minutes of steaming kraut rock. And that's their description, not mine.
As laid down at last year's Reading Festival by Europe's most solid metal-minded outfit, the Scorpions.
And we've got new releases from Saxon, Mithra, Gillan, Samson, Queen and Gentle Giant, and classics from Led Zeppelin, Zapper, Sammy Hagar and Crawler, among others.
Which will be found in our Friday Night Connection at time of the week when titillation creeps under your door.
This evening we start with G-Force.
A number 747, Strangers of the Night.
We played it as an album track from Saxon's album Wheels of Steel, but an edited version is going to come out shortly and be their new single.
And I played that especially for Simon Scarrett and David Reynolds,
whom I met outside Broadcasting House here in London earlier on.
They want me to say hello to all their family and friends,
and that's just been done.
Couple of nice fellas, they were.
Also, a quick hello to Quick Jim here,
and Candida and Martin and Phil and Ian and Cy,
and especially Buff and Blute and Ollie and Dobby and Pete.
And this little card comes from Paul Crouch,
who's private, private rather, 035 of the Saxon Militia Guard.
Must say also hello to Spoon and Dutz and Wills,
and all of the Saxon fans who are studying, well, various examinations all around the country.
Best wishes from Nix, who lives in, I think it's Arnside or Avonside in Cumbria.
Alan Fryer wanted me to play some Saxon.
He lives up there in Tiverdale in Walling.
And at the moment, Saxon, of course, are on tour.
Tomorrow night they're at Manchester, and then they do Aberdeen and Edinburgh, rather,
first, then Aberdeen, Glasgow, Wakefield, and they're touring right through to the 14th of June.
Catch them if you can.
And before that, you heard something from a really amazing new album.
It's the new Gary Moore band called G-Force, and that indeed is the title of the album.
That track was called White Knuckles and Rockin' and Rollin'.
10-12, and here's Crawler.
It's been around for quite some time because it was recorded and put out in America way back in 1976.
That was Sammy Hagar singing a Donovan song called Young Girl Blues from the album entitled Nine on a Ten Scale,
which has just been released in this country.
It has, of course, been available on import.
And if you'd like to hear the brand new Sammy Hagar album,
you can hear tracks of it and Sammy himself
on Rock on Saturday tomorrow

I can take about an hour on the tower of power.
